global_notes:
Working from the aggregates only (no beat-by-beat data here), so this is a read of the macro shape rather than specific cuts:

The edit runs brisk — 30 cuts over 43.7s averages ~1.46s per beat, which sits in the pocket of a mid-tempo track and suggests the editor is cutting on-beat rather than holding through phrases. The heavy front-camera lean (80/20 front vs overhead) is a stylistic tell: this editor is prioritizing personality and hand performance over pure instructional clarity, reserving overhead for the moments where geometry actually matters (likely the wrapping/folding technique and maybe plating), and trusting front angles to carry prep and transformation. The 17 "added" beats against 10 from the editorial group means more than half the timeline is the editor's own rhythmic embellishment — extra inserts, reaction beats, or micro-cuts layered on top of the base structural cuts, which typically reads as an editor building density and musicality rather than just hitting the script. That ratio usually signals speed ramps or flash cuts around the technique section and a punchier opener than the template calls for. Without the per-beat verdicts I can't confirm where the acceleration lands, but the shape — front-dominant, cut-dense, heavily augmented — reads as a performance-forward edit rather than a clinical how-to.

beats.beat_description: beauty opener (2 shots): Finished crab rangoon plate with sweet chili dipping sauce → Same plate, tighter framing → Even tighter on plated rangoons

camera.reasoning: Front is the anchor shot here — the hand-holding-a-rangoon hero reveal is the money shot that sells "crab rangoon," while the preceding overhead push-in establishes the plate context; front shows the crosshatch sealing folds and dimensional shape a top-down couldn't.

duration.reasoning: 3.3s holds long enough for the viewer to read the "Crab Rangoon / the kitchn" title card and register the finished dish before the recipe begins — title readability drives the hold, not the hand motion.

in_point.reasoning: 33.8s is where the best take of the hero hand-hold lands — rangoon cleanly above the dip bowl with good rotation/lighting before the action peaks.

beats.effects: Animated zoom in (55.0% -> 70.0%); Animated reframe (position keyframes)
beats.effects_reasoning: The 55%→70% zoom plus reframe is a functional push-in manufacturing movement on an otherwise static plated shot — no organic transformation (no melting, pouring, pooling) is happening, just a timing/energy nudge into the hero cut.

beats.beat_description: ingredient (1 clips): Cream cheese block being scraped from foil wrapper into glass bowl → Cream cheese block sitting in bowl, foil being peeled back → Cream cheese block fully plopped in bowl, wrapper removed
beats.recipe_section: ingredient dump (text overlay marks this step)

camera.reasoning: Overhead because this is the anchor shot for "ingredient lands in bowl" — the action is the cream cheese block hitting the glass bowl, which only a top-down view reveals; front would just show hands above a bowl rim with no visible ingredient, and this also establishes the overhead opening of the dump run.

duration.reasoning: 1.4s matches the "Cream cheese" MOGRT window (3.3s–4.8s) — two short, familiar words that read comfortably in ~1.4s, so the hold is set by text readability, not by how long the scrape takes.

in_point.reasoning: 5.8s lands on the scrape-in moment where the block is actively being pushed off the foil into the bowl — peak transformation frame (ingredient entering container), skipping the pre-roll where the foil is still closed.

beats.effects_reasoning: Punch-in to 64% tightens the frame onto the bowl and cream cheese so the ingredient reads clearly at 1.4s under the overlay, trimming empty counter space around the bowl — functional reframe, no speed ramp present.

beats.beat_description: ingredient (1 clips): Cream cheese block fully plopped in bowl, wrapper removed → Shredded imitation crab in glass bowl, mid-pour/arrange → Imitation crab settled in bowl, hand withdrawing
beats.recipe_section: ingredient dump (text overlay marks this step)

duration.reasoning: 1.4s matches the read time of the "Imitation crab" MOGRT (13 characters) — same hold as the prior "Cream cheese" beat, and the next shorter word "Garlic" drops to 1.2s, confirming text length drives duration.

in_point.reasoning: 19.3s lands on the mid-pour/arrange moment — crab actively streaming/settling into the bowl with the hand still in frame, which is the peak-action frame of that take rather than the static before/after.

beats.beat_description: ingredient (1 clips): Closer angle on shredded imitation crab bowl, small dish of minced garlic about to be added → Minced garlic being tipped from small bowl onto crab → Garlic settled on top of crab pile, bowl lifting away
beats.recipe_section: ingredient dump (text overlay marks this step)

duration.reasoning: 1.2s because "Garlic" is a 6-letter single-word MOGRT that reads almost instantly, so the hold only needs to cover the tip-and-settle action plus minimum legibility — the text overlay running 6.2s–7.3s (1.1s) confirms readability, not action length, set the bound.

in_point.reasoning: 41.7s lands on the pre-tip frame where the small garlic dish is hovering over the crab bowl but hasn't poured yet, so the cut captures the full dump arc (hover → tip → settle → bowl lift) within the 1.2s window rather than starting mid-pour.

beats.beat_description: ingredient (1 clips): Sliced scallions being dumped from small bowl into crab mixture → Scallions piled on top of crab+garlic, wider framing → Soy sauce being poured from small dish onto the pile
beats.recipe_section: ingredient dump (text overlay marks this step)

camera.reasoning: Overhead is a switch point — after two front-angle dumps (crab, garlic), the editor breaks the run to show scallions landing on top of the growing pile, which a front/eye-level angle can't reveal because the stack geometry (scallions on crab on garlic) only reads from above.

duration.reasoning: 1.1s matches how long "Scallions" takes to read — a short word gets a short hold, consistent with MOGRT readability driving duration rather than the dump action itself.

in_point.reasoning: 53.1s catches the mid-dump moment where the donor bowl is still tipped and scallions are actively falling onto the pile, showing both the action and the accumulation in one frame rather than a pre-dump or settled-pile state.

beats.effects_reasoning: Punch-in to 62% tightens on the bowl-and-pile to make the small scallion volume read clearly against the wider crab/garlic base — this is a functional reframe for legibility, not an organic transformation, and no speed ramp is present.

beats.beat_description: ingredient (1 clips): Soy sauce being poured from small dish onto the pile → Soy sauce finishing pour, puddling on scallions → Small bowl of sugar being tipped in
beats.recipe_section: ingredient dump (text overlay marks this step)

duration.reasoning: 1.1s is the read time for the "Soy sauce" MOGRT — two short words with a space, standard ingredient-label hold, not tied to pour duration.

in_point.reasoning: 62.8s lands mid-pour with the dark stream already visible and puddling starting — skips the dead pre-pour setup so the ingredient reads instantly when the cut hits.

beats.beat_description: ingredient (1 clips): Small bowl of sugar being tipped in → White granulated sugar mounded on top of mix → Toasted sesame oil being drizzled from small pitcher

duration.reasoning: 1.1s gives just enough screen time to read the three-word "Toasted sesame oil" MOGRT before cutting; ingredient dumps in this run sit in the 1.1–1.2s band for that reason.

in_point.reasoning: 73.1s lands on the sugar-tipping moment with the bowl already inverted and grains falling, skipping the dead approach frames so the cut opens on visible motion.

beats.effects: punch-in/crop (62.0% vs 50% baseline); Reframed (offset h:0.04 v:0.06)
beats.effects_reasoning: Punch-in to 62% tightens the frame onto the bowl/pour zone so the small pitcher and stream read clearly against the busy mise-en-place, and the small h:0.04 v:0.06 reframe re-centers the action after the crop — functional framing tool, not an organic flourish (no speed ramp present).

beats.beat_description: ingredient (1 clips): Toasted sesame oil being drizzled from small pitcher → Sesame oil pooling over sugar and scallions → Sesame oil drizzle continuing, smaller stream
beats.recipe_section: ingredient dump (text overlay marks this step)

duration.reasoning: 1.2s matches the "Toasted sesame oil" MOGRT overlay window (10.7s–11.9s) — the hold equals how long the two-word label needs to be read, not the pour action itself.

in_point.reasoning: 79.4s lands right as the amber stream leaves the pitcher and begins pooling, giving a clean "action starts now" frame that syncs with the text overlay popping on.

beats.effects: punch-in/crop (62.0% vs 50% baseline); Reframed (offset h:0.04 v:0.06)
beats.effects_reasoning: Tighter 62% crop with a small h:0.04/v:0.06 reframe pushes the pitcher and pooling oil toward frame center so the drizzle reads at this short duration — functional reframe, no speed ramp present.

beats.beat_description: ingredient (1 clips): Garlic powder being sprinkled onto pile from a pinch bowl → Garlic powder settled, hand withdrawing → Ground white pepper being tapped over mound from small shaker

duration.reasoning: 1.2s — "Garlic powder" is a two-word, 13-character MOGRT; 1.2s is the minimum comfortable read for that string, matching the adjacent ingredient beats (1.2–1.3s).

in_point.reasoning: 90.3s — lands on active sprinkle (pale yellow powder mid-fall from the pinch bowl), not the settled/hand-withdrawing frame, so the dump reads as a live action rather than a static product shot.

beats.beat_description: ingredient (1 clips): Ground white pepper being tapped over mound from small shaker → White pepper continuing, more dusting visible → Seasoned mound at rest before mixing
beats.recipe_section: ingredient dump (text overlay marks this step)

duration.reasoning: 1.3s matches the read time for the three-word "Ground white pepper" MOGRT — long enough to read, consistent with the ~1.2s neighbor beat, and the dusting action itself fills the hold without feeling stretched.

in_point.reasoning: 94.5s lands on the active tap where pepper is visibly falling from the shaker onto the mound — entering mid-action avoids a static pre-tap frame and delivers the ingredient reveal the moment the overlay appears.

beats.beat_description: Both hands holding bowl, spatula beginning to fold ingredients

camera.reasoning: backfilled 2026-05-02: nested-sequence wrapper, no single source camera

duration.reasoning: 1.9s is just long enough for the speed-ramped nest to actually show the mixture transforming from streaky to combined without a flash-cut, while keeping the mix-combine action compressed so it doesn't stall the section.

in_point.reasoning: 0.0s — the source begins exactly at "both hands holding bowl, spatula beginning to fold," which is the first frame of the transformation; any earlier in-point would be pre-mix setup with nothing changing.

beats.effects: Animated zoom in (110.0% -> 120.0%); Reframed (offset h:-0.50 v:-0.50)

beats.beat_description: 3/4 angle on bowl, spatula lifting a portion of combined mix

duration.reasoning: 1.7s is a quick "button" beat that punctuates the end of the mixing phase — long enough to register the finished filling's texture (pale pink/green paste flecked with scallion/crab), short enough to feel like a transition into the wrapping section that follows.

in_point.reasoning: 170.3s catches the spatula mid-lift with a portion raised out of the bowl, which is the single frame that best reveals the finished consistency — starting any later would land on the smashing/smoothing action, which shows process rather than result.

beats.beat_description: ingredient (1 clips): First wonton wrapper being placed on teal cutting board; small water bowl in upper-left → First wrapper aligned on board, hand withdrawing → Two wrappers now laid out, hands gone (compilation state 1)
beats.recipe_section: ingredient dump (text overlay marks this step)

camera.reasoning: backfilled 2026-05-02: nested-sequence wrapper, no single source camera

duration.reasoning: 2.5s fits two back-to-back MOGRTs ("Wonton wrappers" 17.9–19.3s then "Crab mixture" 19.3–20.3s) plus the sealing callout beat — the read time of both labels drives the hold, and the 13 compilation states need breathing room to register as stop-motion rather than a jitter.

in_point.reasoning: 0.0s starts at the nest head where the first wrapper lands on the teal board, syncing the visual onset with the "Wonton wrappers" text overlay fire at 17.9s so the label and the object arrive together.

beats.effects: Animated zoom in (104.0% -> 121.0%); Animated reframe (position keyframes)
beats.effects_reasoning: Animated zoom (104→121%) + reframe keyframes are functional — ken-burns motion keeping a static overhead alive across 13 near-identical compilation states and recentering as the wrapper row grows leftward-to-rightward; no speed ramp, so these are edit-tool sweetening for a held camera, not an organic flourish.

beats.beat_description: ingredient (3 clips): Same close-up; one finger pressing near filling as wrapper starts to close around it → Tighter angle on sealing the wrapper around filling; wrapper pulled up and over → Two hands lifting wrapper corners up over filling
beats.recipe_section: ingredient dump (text overlay marks this step)

duration.reasoning: The "Trace the border with a finger dipped in water" callout plus the mogrt instructional overlay runs roughly 20.3s–23.6s (~3.3s) — text readability on the instruction drives the hold, and three sub-actions (press, pull, lift) need time to register.

in_point.reasoning: At 188.4s the finger is just initiating pressure near the filling as the wrapper begins to fold over — the cut lands on the start of the seal, immediately after the callout establishes the technique.

beats.effects_reasoning: Punch-in to 63% tightens on the finger/wrapper contact so the sealing detail reads while the callout box eats screen real estate — functional reframe for attended detail work, not an organic transformation.

beats.beat_description: Rangoon fully pinched into 4-point star shape, hands shaping edges
Close-up, hands holding in-progress pouch of filling, squeezing corners together

duration.reasoning: 1.6s is the longest beat in this three-cut run (1.1s → 1.6s → 1.0s), which makes it the anchor of the mini-sequence — the clearest "pouch being formed" moment gets held longer so the transformation reads, with tighter cuts bracketing it.

in_point.reasoning: 202.9s lands on the hands actively squeezing the corners together on a visibly in-progress pouch — you enter mid-action on the pinch, not on a static hold or a reset, which is the most legible "forming" frame in the take.

beats.beat_description: Final shaped rangoon on board, hands pressing top corners inward

duration.reasoning: 1.0s is a short punctuation beat sandwiched between two longer front holds (1.6s and 1.4s) — just enough to register the corner-press action without lingering, keeping the held-camera run from going stale.

in_point.reasoning: 218.8s lands exactly on hands pressing top corners inward on the finished rangoon — a clean, complete shaping gesture, before the hands pull back at 27.0s into a transitional moment.

beats.beat_description: Hands pressing on fresh rangoon, shaping top

duration.reasoning: 1.4s is a slight hold (longer than the 1.0s prior and 1.3s next) that lets the press-and-shape gesture read completely before the camera change and batch reveal — enough time to register "the rangoon is finished" so the overhead wide lands as a payoff rather than a non-sequitur.

in_point.reasoning: 225.6s lands right as hands re-engage to press the top (27.5s frame), skipping the 27.0s transition frame where hands are pulling back from the previous rangoon — cutting in on the start of new action, not on dead motion.

beats.beat_description: Wide shot of fully shaped rangoon batch — roughly 4x6 grid of pinwheel rangoons on cutting board

camera.reasoning: Overhead is the only angle that shows the full 4x6 grid — this is the anchor shot for the "finished prep" reveal, and it's a deliberate switch point punctuating the front→front run on either side because a front angle would flatten the batch into an unreadable line.

duration.reasoning: 1.3s is a short reveal beat — nothing is moving in the frame (stationary batch, no hands yet), so the cut just needs long enough to register "whole batch done" before pushing into the fry, and holding longer would stall momentum right as the recipe pivots to cooking.

in_point.reasoning: 17.5s lands on the clean stationary batch before the hand enters from bottom-right at 29.5s timeline — the editor wants the uninterrupted "ta-da" frame, not a frame with a hand already reaching in, so the in-point is chosen to sit in the hands-free window.

beats.beat_description: Cut to frying pot — spider strainer lowering two raw rangoons into hot oil

duration.reasoning: 1.5s covers a complete micro-action arc — lower (30.0s), submerge with heavy bubbling (30.5s), strainer lifting away (31.0s) — cutting at 31.5s hands off to overhead exactly when the strainer has cleared frame and there's nothing more for this angle to show.

in_point.reasoning: 12.3s lands on the rangoons crossing the oil surface with pale uncooked wrappers still visible — starting any earlier wastes time on empty approach, any later misses the "raw goes in" moment that motivates cutting away from the previous overhead.

beats.beat_description: Top-down on fry pot — four rangoons floating in amber oil, golden-brown

camera.reasoning: Overhead is the anchor here — it's the only angle that shows all four rangoons at once floating in the amber oil with bubbles ringing each one, which is what sells "they're frying"; a front/eye-level shot would show the pot rim and maybe one rangoon's top, not the pattern.

duration.reasoning: 1.1s is shorter than its 1.5s/1.2s front neighbors because the frame reads instantly (four brown shapes in bubbling oil) — the editor gets the info across and cuts before the static float becomes boring, keeping the frying sequence moving.

in_point.reasoning: 96.9s lands on rangoons already golden-brown and floating with active bubbling around the edges — past the pale-dough stage, before the strainer lift, so the frame reads as "mid-fry, transforming" without ambiguity.

beats.effects_reasoning: Punch-in to 66% tightens onto the four rangoons and bubbling oil, cropping out dead pot-rim space so the 1.1s beat isn't wasted on negative space — functional reframe for a brief insert, not a speed-ramped transformation moment.

beats.beat_description: Spider strainer holds two finished golden-brown rangoons up out of oil

duration.reasoning: 1.2s gives the eye just enough time to register "raw dough → finished rangoon" without lingering past the lift's peak, fitting the building cadence between the 1.1s overhead before and the 1.6s salting shot after.

in_point.reasoning: 20.0s lands exactly as the strainer clears the oil with the rangoons held up — the peak reveal frame, before the lift settles and after the draining motion has begun.

beats.beat_description: ingredient (1 clips): Rack of fried golden rangoons on wire rack being salted (falling salt grains visible) → Rack of rangoons, more salt grains falling → Rack of rangoons, salt flakes visible on top edges

in_point.reasoning: 14.9s — picked the frame where salt grains are mid-air and clearly visible falling onto the rack, i.e. the peak of the action rather than the pre-sprinkle or post-settle moments.

beats.beat_description: Rack of rangoons, same angle, held

duration.reasoning: 1.6s matches the prior ingredient beat's 1.6s and gives the "Kosher salt" MOGRT enough on-screen time to read before the cut lands on the plating switch at [36.0s].

in_point.reasoning: 46.0s picks the steady, settled frame after the salt has landed — rack still, flakes visible, no hand in frame — which is the clean read the overlay needs.

beats.beat_description: Tight macro on cluster of golden rangoons, shallow DOF on center rangoon

duration.reasoning: 1.3s matches the beauty-shot cadence of the surrounding plating beats (1.6s front → 1.3s macro → 1.4s overhead), long enough to register the hero rangoon's texture but short enough to stay inside the musical phrase before handing to the overhead serving moment.

in_point.reasoning: 15.9s is the settled frame where focus has locked onto the center rangoon with the shallow DOF fully resolved — earlier frames in the source likely have focus hunting or micro-movement, and this is the first moment the shot is clean.

beats.beat_description: Same macro, holds

camera.reasoning: Overhead because the plated-rangoon top-down with dip bowl upper-left and two hands reaching in from opposite corners is the best-take anchor for the plating reveal — front couldn't show the full plate composition, the bowl placement, or the symmetric multi-hand gesture that sells "ready to share."

duration.reasoning: 1.4s is long enough to register the plated composition and the two hands entering/gripping, then cut before the action stalls — the dip hero shot needs to land next.

in_point.reasoning: 36.2s is the frame where both hands are mid-reach into the plate from opposing corners, giving the symmetric "served" composition that makes the shot read instantly.

beats.effects_reasoning: The 64%→55% zoom out with a tiny vertical reframe is a functional push-pull that reveals more plate/hands as the beat plays and nudges the composition — no speed ramp, attended hand action, not an organic flourish.

beats.beat_description: Hand dipping a single rangoon into sweet chili sauce bowl

duration.reasoning: 4.0s holds long because this is the closing beauty moment carrying the "the kitchn" logo reveal and end-card landing — the shot has to breathe through dip → submerge → lift → drip → final pose.

in_point.reasoning: 19.7s catches the rangoon just as it enters the sauce, giving the full dunk-lift-drip arc in one unbroken take rather than starting mid-action.

beats.effects_reasoning: Animated zoom out 57→50% is functional — a slow pull-back that opens the frame for the logo overlay and end-card feeling, nudging the shot from tight action to wider beauty composition; no speed ramp present.
